note: #529 — the connection-budget accounting the 2026-04-21 outage lacked. gate:db-budget computes Σ(helm replicas × per-service pool ceiling) + reserved and FAILS if it exceeds max_connections. Ceilings default to pool_size 5 + max_overflow 10 = 15; #635 wired these as env knobs (DB_POOL_SIZE/DB_MAX_OVERFLOW) that admin-api and meeting-api now READ and apply to their async engines (defaults match this file), so an operator can tune per-pod pools to fit a managed-Postgres max_connections — reconcile any override here. The gate still audits the static default worst case and refuses a budget that UNDER-states any explicit literal pool_size/max_overflow in a service's code. This budget is the DIRECT-connection worst case (pgbouncer OFF); with pgbouncer transaction-mode on (deploy/helm values pgbouncer.enabled=true) the server-side slot count is decoupled from these per-pod pools.
